Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor

Class CborSerializer

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.OutputStreamSerializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor.CborSerializer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Function<Object,byte[]>, ThrowingFunction<Object,byte[]>, CborMetaProvider, ArrayRecordWritable, RecordWritable, TokenWritable
Direct Known Subclasses:
CborSerializer.Base64, CborSerializer.SpacedHex
public class CborSerializer extends OutputStreamSerializer implements CborMetaProvider, TokenWritable, ArrayRecordWritable
Serializes POJO models to CBOR (RFC 8949).
Media types:
Handles Accept types: application/cbor

Produces Content-Type types: application/cbor

Description

Java-to-CBOR type mapping:

  • Maps and beans → CBOR map (major type 5)
  • Collections and arrays → CBOR array (major type 4)
  • String / Enum → CBOR text string (major type 3, UTF-8)
  • Boolean → CBOR simple values 20/21 (0xF4/0xF5)
  • Number (int, long, float, double) → CBOR compact integers (major types 0/1) or IEEE 754 floats (major type 7)
  • Date / Calendar / Instant → ISO 8601 string
  • Duration → ISO 8601 duration string
  • byte[] → CBOR byte string (major type 2)
  • null → CBOR simple value 22 (0xF6)
  • All other types → fallback to toString() as string
Behavior-specific subclasses:
  • CborSerializer.SpacedHex — Spaced-hex text output for debugging
  • CborSerializer.Base64 — Base64 text output
Example:

// Serialize a bean to CBOR bytes using the default serializer byte[] cbor = CborSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(myBean); // Custom serializer with bean type properties enabled CborSerializer s = CborSerializer.create().addBeanTypes().build(); byte[] cbor = s.serialize(myBean);

Round-trip notes:
  • BigInteger — emitted losslessly as a native CBOR integer when it fits CBOR's integer range (-2^64 .. 2^64-1), otherwise as a lossless decimal string.
  • BigDecimal — emitted as a lossless decimal string (native CBOR has no decimal number head without semantic tags).
  • The high-level databind path emits definite-length containers; the streaming CborTokenWriter emits indefinite-length containers (both are valid RFC 8949).
  • Half-precision float (0xF9) not produced; always uses float32 (0xFA) or float64 (0xFB).
  • Semantic tags (major type 6) not emitted unless useTags(true).
Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
See Also:
  • CBOR Basics
  • RFC 8949

    • copy

      public CborSerializer.Builder copy()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Creates a builder from this context object.

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      Overrides:
      copy in class Serializer
      Returns:
      A new Builder object.

    • createSession

      public CborSerializerSession.Builder createSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class OutputStreamSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • getSession

      public CborSerializerSession getSession()

      Returns a session to use for this context.

      Note that subclasses may opt to return a reusable non-modifiable session.

      Overrides:
      getSession in class OutputStreamSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session object.

    • serializeTokens

      public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator that opens a CborTokenWriter over the output using default session arguments (mirrors OutputStreamSerializer.serialize(Object)).

      The real implementation lives on CborSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object). Callers that need request-derived configuration (locale, timezone, schema, swaps) should call createSession() and invoke CborSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object) on the built session instead.

      Specified by:
      serializeTokens in interface TokenWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output. Accepts OutputStream or File.
      Returns:
      A new CborTokenWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.

    • isAddBeanTypes

      protected final boolean isAddBeanTypes()
      Description copied from class: Serializer
      Add "_type" properties when needed.
      Overrides:
      isAddBeanTypes in class Serializer
      Returns:
      true if "_type" properties added to beans if their type cannot be inferred through reflection.
      See Also:
      • Serializer.Builder.addBeanTypes()

    • serializeArrayRecords

      public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the streaming array-element RecordWriter (uses default session args; see serializeTokens(Object)). Real impl on CborSerializerSession.serializeArrayRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      serializeArrayRecords in interface ArrayRecordWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new element-streamed RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
